circuitscaper 0.1.0
  • Reference
  • Articles
    • Getting Started with circuitscaper

All-to-One Circuitscape Analysis

Source: R/cs-all-to-one.R
cs_all_to_one.Rd

For each focal node, all other nodes are sources and that node is the ground. Computes effective resistance and current flow maps.

cs_all_to_one(
  resistance,
  locations,
  resistance_is = "resistances",
  four_neighbors = FALSE,
  solver = "cg+amg",
  output_dir = NULL,
  verbose = FALSE
)

Arguments

resistance

A terra::SpatRaster or file path. The resistance (or conductance) surface.

locations

A terra::SpatRaster or file path. Focal nodes/regions raster with integer IDs.

resistance_is

Character. Whether the resistance surface represents "resistances" (default) or "conductances".

four_neighbors

Logical. Use 4-neighbor (rook) connectivity instead of 8-neighbor (queen). Default FALSE.

solver

Character. Solver to use: "cg+amg" (default) or "cholmod".

output_dir

Optional character path. If provided, output files persist there. Default NULL uses a temporary directory that is cleaned up automatically.

verbose

Logical. Print Circuitscape solver output. Default FALSE.

Value

A terra::SpatRaster with named layers for each output map, including per-node current maps and a cumulative current map.

References

Circuitscape user guide: https://docs.circuitscape.org/Circuitscape.jl/latest/usage/

See also

cs_pairwise(), cs_one_to_all(), cs_advanced(), cs_setup()

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
library(terra)
res <- rast(nrows = 10, ncols = 10, vals = runif(100, 1, 10))
locs <- rast(nrows = 10, ncols = 10, vals = 0)
locs[1, 1] <- 1; locs[1, 10] <- 2; locs[10, 5] <- 3
result <- cs_all_to_one(res, locs)
plot(result)
} # }
